food_subgroup: Coffee-based beverages — drip-brewed robusta coffee with sweetened condensed milk, served over ice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Ca Phe Sua Da (Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk). FOODON_03315674 (iced coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GFQ is the closest FoodEx2 category; no specific code exists for Vietnamese phin-brewed robusta coffee with condensed milk.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific beverage preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of Ca Phe Sua Da is approximately 200 ml (effective liquid volume after ice dilution), yielding approximately 155 kcal, 2.6 g protein, 3.0 g fat, and 22 g sugar — all sugar derived from the sweetened condensed milk.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Sweetened Condensed Milk (FDC ID 1097512) and Brewed Robusta Coffee (FDC ID 171890); composite recipe calculation for Ca Phe Sua Da
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Ca Phe Sua Da as a composite dish. Values are derived from a recipe-level calculation: ~40 g sweetened condensed milk (USDA FDC 1097512) + ~160 ml brewed robusta coffee (USDA FDC 171890 as closest approved proxy) + ice (negligible nutrient contribution), yielding per-100 g values as reported. Robusta-specific caffeine data (~80 mg per 200 ml serving) sourced from peer-reviewed literature on Coffea canephora composition. All values cross-checked against the input record's per_100g figures, which are consistent with this recipe calculation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweetened Condensed Milk and Sucrose/Lactose Composite — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for condensed milk (GI ~61) and sucrose (GI ~65) / lactose (GI ~46) blend; milk fat moderates postprandial glucose response
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (glycemicindex.com) — analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ca Phe Sua Da as a composite beverage. GI ~55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened condensed milk (GI ~61, Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) and the blended sugar profile (sucrose GI ~65, lactose GI ~46). Milk fat in condensed milk moderates the glycaemic response, supporting a composite GI estimate of ~55. GL per 200 ml serving = (55 × 22 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 12.1. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (~75 mg per 200 ml serving); transient caffeine-related blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Ca Phe Sua Da contains only ~75 mg sodium per glass (very low), posing no meaningful sodium-related hypertension risk; however, robusta coffee delivers ~80 mg caffeine per glass, which may cause a transient systolic blood pressure rise of 3–8 mmHg for 1–3 hours in caffeine-naive or caffeine-sensitive hypertensive patients — regular coffee consumers show largely attenuated acute pressor response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 22 g sugar per 200 ml serving entirely from sweetened condensed milk; GI ~55, GL ~12.1 per glass
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ca Phe Sua Da delivers 22 g of sugar per glass (equivalent to 5.5 teaspoons) entirely from sweetened condensed milk — a hidden glycaemic load frequently underreported in dietary recall as 'just coffee'; T2DM patients should switch to cà phê đen đá (black iced coffee, ~10 kcal, 0 g sugar, same ~80 mg caffeine) or reduce condensed milk to 20 g per glass to halve the sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed milk (~2 g per serving); high sugar load; coffee polyphenol benefits partially offset by sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each glass provides ~2 g saturated fat and 22 g added sugar from condensed milk, contributing to dyslipidaemia and triglyceride elevation risk with regular consumption; robusta coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) have documented cardioprotective associations in epidemiological studies, but this benefit is substantially offset by the high sugar content — patients with CVD should limit to occasional consumption or substitute black iced coffee.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory effect of 22 g sugar per serving partially offsets anti-inflammatory coffee polyphenols
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The 22 g sugar per glass promotes postprandial hyperglycaemia and pro-inflammatory cytokine release, partially negating the anti-inflammatory benefit of robusta coffee polyphenols; RA patients who wish to consume Vietnamese coffee should switch to black iced coffee to preserve the anti-inflammatory benefit without the sugar-driven inflammatory stimulus.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ca Phe Sua Da (Cà Phê Sữa Đá) is Vietnamese iced coffee — one of the world's most distinctive coffee preparations. Dark-roasted Vietnamese robusta coffee is slowly drip-brewed through a phin (a small gravity-fed stainless steel filter) directly over a layer of sweetened condensed milk in a glass, then poured over ice. The result is an intensely strong, bittersweet coffee concentrate transformed into a rich, creamy, and very sweet iced beverage. Robusta coffee (Coffea canephora) is used almost exclusively in Vietnamese coffee culture — it has approximately twice the caffeine content of arabica (~80 mg per 200 ml glass) and a characteristically bold, earthy, low-acidity flavour. The condensed milk substitutes for fresh milk, which was scarce in pre-refrigeration Vietnam, and was introduced during French colonial influence in Indochina. Per 200 ml glass: approximately 155 kcal, 2.6 g protein, 3.0 g fat (2.0 g saturated), 22 g carbohydrate (22 g sugar — entirely from condensed milk), ~75 mg sodium, GI ~55, GL ~12.1. CLINICAL NOTE: the 22 g sugar per glass is a hidden glycaemic load — patients frequently describe this beverage as 'just coffee' in dietary recall, missing the equivalent of 5.5 teaspoons of sugar. For T2DM patients: cà phê đen đá (black iced coffee) delivers the same ~80 mg caffeine with ~0 g sugar and ~10 kcal. Ca Phe Sua Da is the most ordered beverage at Vietnamese restaurants and cafés in Singapore. Source: USDA FoodData Central (condensed milk + brewed coffee composite).

how much sugar in Vietnamese iced coffee

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Ca Phe Sua Da as a composite dish. Values are derived from a recipe-level calculation using USDA FoodData Central entries for sweetened condensed milk (FDC 1097512) and brewed robusta coffee (FDC 171890 as closest proxy), which is an adapted rather than exact match. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for condensed milk and its constituent sugars. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite beverage (robusta coffee + condensed milk) are not separately reported in USDA FoodData Central at the recipe level; individual condensed milk fatty acid data exist but were not disaggregated for this composite. water_g is set to -1: total water content of the composite beverage (including ice melt contribution) is not reported as a discrete value in the approved source. 2. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 — confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central condensed milk entry (FDC 1097512). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero for a liquid coffee-and-condensed-milk beverage with no plant fibre components. 3. GI ~55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened condensed milk and its constituent sugars (sucrose GI ~65, lactose GI ~46); no direct GI measurement for Ca Phe Sua Da exists in the published literature. GL per 200 ml serving = (55 × 22 g) / 100 = 12.1. 4. Robusta caffeine content (~80 mg per 200 ml serving) is consistent with peer-reviewed literature on Coffea canephora composition but is not captured in the per_100g nutrient table as caffeine is not a standard USDA FoodData Central nutrient field in this template. 5. Two portion sizes are noted in the input: 200 g (effective volume after ice dilution) and 250 g (full liquid volume). The 200 g default is used for GL calculation. 6. Vietnamese cuisine multilingual rule satisfied: Vietnamese native-script name (Cà Phê Sữa Đá) is present. Chinese, Malay, and Tamil aliases are included for Singapore-context retrieval relevance. Hindi local_name is null — no established Hindi-language name for this Vietnamese beverage is in common use in Singapore.
